headline: Consumer Tech News (Jan 20-Jan 24): Trump Revokes Biden's AI Policies, Meta Launches New 'Edits' App To Lure TikTok Users & More
summary: Trump revoked Biden's AI executive order as one of his first actions in office. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman expressed support for Trump, while EA's revenue declined due to slowdown in football. Instagram launched a new app to compete with TikTok and Perplexity reportedly bids to merge with TikTok.

headline: Benzinga Bulls And Bears: Amazon, Tesla, Meta And Dogecoin ETF Hopes Rise With New Fund Filing
summary: Benzinga examined the prospects for many investors’ favorite stocks over the last week — here’s a look at some of our top stories.

headline: Amazon Prime Video Shifts Focus To Live Sports, Looks To Become Profitable By End Of 2025
summary: Prime Video to focus on live sports to reach profitability by 2025. This strategic move aligns with CEO Andy Jassy's vision.

headline: Apple Stock Nears Critical Level Amid Worst Start To The Year Since 2008: 'When You Get Names Starting To Flirt With It...You Kind Of Lose Confidence'
summary: Apple has had a turbulent beginning to 2025, with its shares approaching a significant level that could signal further losses if breached.

headline: Apple Shakes Up AI Division, Taps Veteran Kim Vorrath To Bolster Siri
summary: Apple Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL) has taken a major step to strengthen its AI and Siri capabilities by making a notable leadership change. The company has appointed Kim Vorrath, a veteran executive with years of experience at Apple, as a senior leader in its artificial intelligence and machine learning division.

headline: Apple's iPhone 17 Dynamic Island To Remain 'Largely Unchanged' Despite Prior Rumors, Says Top Analyst
summary: A prominent Apple Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL) analyst on Friday contradicted earlier rumors about the iPhone 17's Dynamic Island, suggesting that it will remain largely unchanged.

headline: Meta's Threads To Introduce Ads In US, Japan As It Looks To Monetize Rival Of Elon Musk's X
summary: Meta is testing ads on its social media platform Threads in the US and Japan. This capitalizes on its rapid growth and user migration from X.

headline: Michigan's Auto Industry Remains Optimistic Despite Trump Revoking Biden's EV Mandate: 'The Investments Aren't Ill Spent'
summary: Michigan's electric vehicle industry is maintaining an optimistic outlook despite recent policy changes announced by President Donald Trump.

headline: Mark Zuckerberg's $65 Billion AI Bet Benefits Nvidia And Other Players, Says Top Analyst, But Warns Market Bull Run Will 'End In A Spectacular Bubble Burst'
summary: Deepwater Asset Management's Gene Munster says Meta Platform Inc.'s (NASDAQ: META) plans to invest up to $65 billion this year to expand its AI infrastructure will benefit Nvidia and other hardware players.

headline: Target Ends DEI Program Following Trump's Call To Eliminate 'Illegal Preferences': Expert Calls It 'Brand Suicide' For Retail Giant With Diverse Customer Base
summary: Target has decided to discontinue its DEI program, becoming the latest in a series of U.S. companies to do so.

headline: Elon Musk Accuses OpenAI CEO Sam Altman Of Lying Over $10B Equity: 'What A Liar'
summary: Tesla Inc CEO Elon Musk earlier this week leveled allegations against OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, accusing him of misleading statements about a $10 billion equity agreement.

headline: Cybertruck Off Road Armor Launch Delayed To March-End, Says Tesla Exec
summary: Tesla executive Siddhant Awasthi said on Thursday that the company is now looking to launch accessories for the Cybertruck including an off-road armor and rocker by the end of March, later than the previously offered timeline.

headline: Researcher Expects Tesla's Vehicle Deliveries To Remain Flat In 2025, Contrary To Musk's 20-30% Growth Prediction
summary: EV giant Tesla Inc will realistically deliver 1.8 million vehicles this year, more or less in line with its deliveries in 2024, according to researcher Troy Teslike.

headline: Apple Co-Founder Steve Jobs In 1981 Said Homes Weren't Ready For Personal Computers — Three Years Later, Cupertino Launched The Macintosh And Made History
summary: In 1981, Steve Jobs doubted the viability of personal computers for home use, but Apple's launch of the Macintosh in 1984 revolutionized computing, setting the foundation for the company's dominance in consumer technology.
